docs.title: Drone Battery Monte Carlo
docs.text:
This project compares drone battery options using a Monte Carlo simulation model under varying mission conditions (e.g., payload, wind, and temperature). It includes:

  • A simulation engine that estimates energy use and mission success
  • A desktop GUI for running comparisons and viewing plots
  • Published API documentation generated by Doxygen

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text:
• API docs: https://jmo-ops.github.io/drone-battery-monte-carlo/
  • GUI page: GUI (use the navigation menu)
  • Logic/model page: Logic (use the navigation menu)

docs.title: 1) Create / activate your environment
docs.text:
If you already have a .venv, activate it:

```bash ..venv\Scripts\activate

docs.title: GUI
docs.text: The project includes a desktop GUI (Tkinter + matplotlib) that lets you run Monte Carlo simulations and compare two battery configurations across typical and extreme mission conditions.

docs.title: 1) Simulation
docs.text:
Run the standard Monte Carlo simulation to compare Battery A vs Battery B under nominal sampling assumptions. The GUI produces:

  • Text summary of results (success rate, remaining energy stats, operational averages)
  • Distribution plots (remaining energy histograms)

docs.title: 2) Parameters Summary
docs.text: Shows the active parameters and model settings used during the run (battery parameters, mission config, penalties, and sampling ranges).

docs.title: 3) Extreme Conditions
docs.text: Runs a deliberately biased Monte Carlo intended to stress the mission (high wind, low temperature, high payload, and slightly longer distance).

docs.title: Extreme simulation settings
docs.text:
• Number of runs per battery (example shown: 500)
  • Biases:
  • Wind: high-biased (10–20 mph)
  • Temp: low-biased (10–95 °F)
  • Payload: high-biased (2.5–5 lb)
  • Distance factor: high-biased (1.0–1.1)

docs.title: Example results (Extreme Conditions)
docs.text: Below is an example output captured from the GUI.

docs.title: Li-ion (NMC, cylindrical) — EXTREME Monte Carlo Results
docs.text:
• Total simulation trials: 500
  • Successful mission trials (met reserve requirement): 500 / 500 (100.0% success probability)

Remaining Energy Distribution - Mean remaining energy: 32.1% - 5th percentile remaining: 16.5% - 95th percentile remaining: 46.8%

Operational Averages - Avg miles (all trials): 16.01 mi - Avg payload (all trials): 4.16 lb - Avg miles (successful only): 16.01 mi - Avg payload (successful only): 4.16 lb

Energy Use & Deliveries - Avg energy used per mission: 168.3 Wh - Missions per charge (float): 1.60 - Approx. deliveries per full charge (floor): 1

Extreme input bias - Wind: high-biased (10–20 mph) - Temp: low-biased (10–95 °F) - Payload: high-biased (2.5–5 lb) - Distance factor: high-biased (1.0–1.1)

docs.title: LiPo (pouch, high-power) — EXTREME Monte Carlo Results
docs.text:
• Total simulation trials: 500
  • Successful mission trials (met reserve requirement): 500 / 500 (100.0% success probability)

Remaining Energy Distribution - Mean remaining energy: 29.4% - 5th percentile remaining: 16.7% - 95th percentile remaining: 42.1%

Operational Averages - Avg miles (all trials): 16.01 mi - Avg payload (all trials): 4.18 lb - Avg miles (successful only): 16.01 mi - Avg payload (successful only): 4.18 lb

Energy Use & Deliveries - Avg energy used per mission: 158.3 Wh - Missions per charge (float): 1.48 - Approx. deliveries per full charge (floor): 1

Extreme input bias - Wind: high-biased (10–20 mph) - Temp: low-biased (10–95 °F) - Payload: high-biased (2.5–5 lb) - Distance factor: high-biased (1.0–1.1)

docs.title: Li-ion vs LiPo comparison (Extreme)
docs.text:
• Δ Mean remaining (Li-ion - LiPo): +2.66%
  • Δ 5th pct remaining (Li-ion - LiPo): -0.14%
  • Δ 95th pct remaining (Li-ion - LiPo): +4.70%
  • Δ Avg Wh/mission (Li-ion - LiPo): +9.94 Wh
  • Δ Missions/charge (Li-ion - LiPo): +0.13
  • Success rate A: 100.0% | Success rate B: 100.0%

Interpretation (for this run): - Both batteries met the reserve requirement in all trials. - Li-ion shows a slightly higher average and upper-tail remaining energy under extreme-biased sampling. - LiPo used slightly less energy per mission in this run, but had lower remaining-energy margin overall.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• If plots don’t appear, verify Tkinter and matplotlib are installed and you’re running in a desktop environment.
  • If the GUI opens but buttons do nothing, run from a terminal to capture tracebacks: ```bash python batteryV2.py
